code.text: Specimen information

text.div:
Specimen information

Test07-FEB-2024Specimen
Appearance of UrineCloudy urineUrine
Odor of UrineUrine smell ammoniacalUrine
Clarity of UrineTranslucentUrine
Color of UrineBrown colorUrine

Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows elevated serum creatinine, suggesting impaired renal function, and high urine protein and ketones, indicating possible nephropathy and ketosis. Urine is cloudy, brown, with low specific gravity, and contains glucose and bilirubin, raising concern for renal and/or hepatic dysfunction. Hemoglobin A1c is low, possibly due to anemia or recent blood loss. No evidence of urinary infection. Recommend further evaluation of renal and hepatic status and assessment for underlying metabolic or hematologic disorders.
